channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Millions of solar power systems could be at risk of cyber attacks after researchers find flurry of vulnerabilities
item.description: Hackers could exploit weak security in solar inverters, manipulating energy production, stealing user data, and even disrupting entire power networks with alarming ease.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to sync iPhone and iPad – iCloud, Photos, Calendars, and more
item.description: One of the big perks of buying an Apple product is the iCloud ecosystem, which keeps everything updated and connected across your devices – here’s how to sync your iPhone and iPad.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Disney+ in April 2025: Andor season 2, Doctor Who season 15, Dying for Sex, and more
item.description: From the return of hugely popular shows to new movies, here's what you'll want to watch on Disney+ in April.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: How to free up space on your Mac
item.description: If you need to free up space on your Mac, consider deleting duplicate photos in your image library. Apple makes it easy to do, and the CyberGuy explains the process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk tries to rehab DOGE after backlash, mistakes
item.description: Elon Musk is pushing to improve the image of his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), suggesting the Tesla CEO is aware of the boogeyman persona he has created for himself and his cost-cutting push since joining President Trump’s team.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: New 3D technology could soon bring surgeons closer to patients in Africa's most remote regions
item.description: A groundbreaking initiative could soon be bringing surgeons closer to patients in Africa's remote corners where the doctor-to-patients ratio is among the lowest in the world.
item.dc:creator: Naa Adorkor Cudjoe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI startup just bought X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday that his xAI has acquired X, the social media app formerly known as Twitter, in an all-stock transaction for $45 billion, including $12 billion in debt. “xAI and X’s futures are intertwined.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Here’s your first hands-on look at the Star Wars: Grogu, Mandalorian, R2-D2, and Darth Vader earbuds
item.description: Star Wars has collaborated with Audio-Technica for four custom earbuds, and TechRadar has your exclusive first-look hands-on experience with all four.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk sells X to his own xAI for $33 billion in all-stock deal
item.description: Elon Musk has sold social media site X to his own xAI artificial intelligence company in a $33 billion all-stock deal, the billionaire announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk says he sold X to his AI company
item.description: Elon Musk on Friday evening announced he has sold his social media company, X, to xAI, his artificial intelligence company, in a deal that values X at $33 billion.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Ubuntu namespace vulnerability should be addressed quickly: Expert
item.description: Linux admins who have enabled the unprivileged user namespace restriction in their recent Ubuntu environments should take action to close three new vulnerabilities that allow a threat actor to bypass the supposed protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Nvidia chip shortage is coming, warns major Chinese server maker
item.description: One of China’s largest server makers, H3C, has flagged potential shortages of Nvidia’s H20 chip, the most advanced AI processor legally available domestically under U.S. export controls, in a client notice seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone that it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: Pietro De Cristofaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Researchers claim their protocol can create truly random numbers on a current quantum computer
item.description: A team that included researchers at a US bank says it has created a protocol that can generate certified truly random numbers, opening the possibility that current generation quantum computers can be used for secure applications in finance, cryptography, cybersecurity, and privacy.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An AI pioneer is launching a blockchain-powered marketplace for open-source models and applications
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: From automation to transformation: How AI is reshaping business
item.description: Are you using artificial intelligence (AI) to do the same things you’ve always done, just more efficiently? If so, you’re only scratching the surface.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: FBI warns about document conversion sites spreading malware
item.description: The FBI warns that some free online document converters load malware onto people's computers, making their private information vulnerable to attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Databricks’ TAO method to allow LLM training with unlabeled data
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has unveiled a new large language model (LLM) training method, TAO that will allow enterprises to train models without labeling data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Gee whiz, Gboard! An invaluable new Android typing upgrade
item.description: Ahh — don’t you just love the feeling of a fresh ‘n’ zesty virtual upgrade? Here in the land o’ Android, that doesn’t mean only major new Android versions (and, alas, the woeful waiting that often accompanies ’em).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vibe coding is groovy
item.description: Vibe coding is most definitely having a moment. Don’t feel bad if you haven’t heard of it—the Wikipedia page for it just went up on March 15 of this year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Open-source Styrolite project aims to simplify container runtime security
item.description: Today Edera launched a new open-source project called Styrolite to bring tighter controls to the interactions between containers and Linux kernel namespaces, at a layer below where Open Container Initiative (OCI) runtimes like containerd operate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How agencies can improve zero trust architecture by addressing their data problem
item.description: Carmelo McCutcheon, the public sector CTO for VAST Data Federal, offers six ways agencies can improve their data protection. The post How agencies can improve zero trust architecture by addressing their data problem first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fauna to shut down FaunaDB service in May
item.description: Fauna, the provider of the NoSQL database FaunaDB, has said that it will shut down the service by the end of May due to the unavailability of capital required to support the database service and market it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GenAI tools for R: New tools to make R programming easier
item.description: My previous article focused on some of the best tools for incorporating LLMs into your R scripts and workflows. We’ll expand on that theme here, offering up a collection of generative AI tools you can use to get help with your R programming or to run LLMs locally with R.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Managing and monitoring user accounts on Linux
item.description: There are a number of commands on Linux that you can use to manage user accounts and monitor user activity. This post provides details on the commands that you need to know if you are managing a Linux server and the user accounts on that system.
item.dc:creator: Network World
